nameMeanings.name: Scott
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Scottish, and Irish (Down): habitational and ethnic name from Middle English Scot ‘man from Scotland’. There is no evidence that the surname denoted either of the earlier senses of Scot as ‘(Gaelic-speaking) Irishman’ or ‘man from Alba’, the Gaelic-speaking region of Scotland north of the river Forth. This surname is also very common among African Americans.
English and Scottish: from the rare Middle English personal name Scot (Old English Scott, possibly also Old Norse Skotr), only certainly attested in northern England.

variants.name: Scutt
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Camille, Pierre.
English: nickname for a short person, from Middle English scut(e) ‘short’, or else for a swift runner, from Middle English scut ‘hare’.

nameMeanings.name: Jean
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Like Jane and Joan , a medieval variant of Old French Je(h)anne. Towards the end of the Middle Ages this form became largely confined to Scotland. In the 20th century it became more widely used in the English-speaking world and enjoyed a period of great popularity, but it is now out of fashion. Among numerous well-known and influential bearers are the British novelists Jean Plaidy ( Eleanor Hibbert , 1910–93 ) and Jean Rhys ( Ella Gwendolen Rees Williams , 1894–1979 ), British actress Jean Simmons ( 1929–2010 ), and American-born actress Jean Seberg ( 1938–79 ). It is also found as a variant spelling of the masculine name Gene .

variants.name: John
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

en.lifeSummary: Jean Scott was born in 1383, in Cockpool, Dumfriesshire, Scotland, United Kingdom. She married Sir Thomas Murray in 1403, in Dumfriesshire, Scotland, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 9 sons and 2 daughters.

fr.lifeSummary: Jean Scott est née en 1383, à Cockpool, Dumfriesshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni. Elle a épousé Sir Thomas Murray en 1403, à Dumfriesshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 9 fils et 2 filles.

de.lifeSummary: Jean Scott wurde 1383 in Cockpool, Dumfriesshire, Schottland, United Kingdom geboren. Sie hat 1403 in Dumfriesshire, Schottland, United Kingdom Sir Thomas Murray geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 9 Söhne und 2 Töchter.

it.lifeSummary: Jean Scott è nata il nel 1383 nel Cockpool, Dumfriesshire, Scozia, Regno Unito. Lei sposato Sir Thomas Murray nel 1403, nel Dumfriesshire, Scozia, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 9 figli e 2 figlie.

ko.lifeSummary: Jean Scott(은)는 1383년 영국 스코틀랜드 드프리실르 콕포에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1403년 영국 스코틀랜드 드프리실르에서 Sir Thomas Murray(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 아홉 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다.
